skill.name: pdf-extract
skill.description: Extract PDF selectable text and full-page or segmented page images (including tables) into Markdown with per-page headings and image links; use when you need both readable text and page visuals for PPT creation, review, or analysis.
skill.summary: Extract PDF text and page images into structured Markdown with per-page headings and image links.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 63 static analysis findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 16 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts matched no cryptographic code. The 37 'shell backtick execution' alerts are documentation examples inside Markdown code blocks. The skill is a legitimate PDF extraction tool that reads user-specified PDFs and writes Markdown output to local files. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or code injection risk was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 16 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across SKILL.md, agents/openai.yaml, scripts/extract_pdf.py, scripts/validate_skill.py, and pdf-extract_audit_result_v2.json. No cryptographic code exists in any of these files. The alerts were triggered by benign text such as descriptions, documentation labels, and Markdown section headings in non-code contexts.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 4 instances of 'system reconnaissance' in pdf-extract_audit_result_v2.json. These are benign evaluation notes from a legacy audit report describing assertion results and output structure validation. No reconnaissance activity exists.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Unicode Escape Sequence
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 4 'unicode escape sequence' occurrences on scripts/extract_pdf.py line 330. These are legitimate Unicode CJK punctuation characters (。, ！, ？, ；) used to split sentences in East Asian text. This is not obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 37 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All instances appear inside Markdown code blocks (```bash or ```text) as documentation examples showing users how to run the Python script. No executable code is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 2 'hardcoded URL' instances on SKILL.md line 7. This is the source attribution URL pointing to the skill's GitHub repository. It is legitimate reference metadata, not suspicious network behavior.

content.user_title: Extract PDF Content to Markdown with Page Images
content.value_statement: PDF files often lock text and images inside a format that is hard to reuse. This skill extracts selectable text and page images into structured Markdown, making the content accessible for analysis, presentations, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Extracts selectable text from PDF files and normalizes it into readable paragraphs
Renders PDF pages as images in three modes: full page, segmented blocks, and embedded images
Filters text-heavy images using OCR to keep only meaningful visuals like tables and diagrams
Generates structured Markdown output with per-page headings and linked image references
Supports intelligent page segmentation to capture tables, figures, and diagrams separately
limitations: Cannot extract text from scanned PDFs that lack a selectable text layer
Image quality depends on the render DPI setting and original PDF resolution
Heading detection uses font-size heuristics and may not work with all PDF layouts
Filtering options require Tesseract OCR to be installed on the local system

use_cases.title: Convert Research Papers to Markdown
use_cases.description: Extract text and figures from academic PDF papers for literature review and note-taking.

use_cases.title: Prepare Presentation Materials
use_cases.description: Extract tables, diagrams, and text from PDF reports for use in slide decks.

use_cases.title: Build Document Analysis Datasets
use_cases.description: Convert a collection of PDF documents into structured Markdown with linked images for downstream data processing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF to Markdown Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text and images from [input.pdf] and save as [output.md].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick extraction of a single PDF file

prompt_templates.title: PDF Extraction with Page Segmentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text and segmented images from [input.pdf]. Use --image-mode segment to capture tables and figures as separate image blocks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extracting a PDF with complex layouts containing tables and figures

prompt_templates.title: PDF Extraction with Text-Image Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract content from [input.pdf] and filter out text-heavy images. Use --filter-text on to remove images that are mostly text, keeping only meaningful visuals like diagrams.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up extraction results by removing redundant text screenshots

prompt_templates.title: Complete PDF Processing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract content from [input.pdf] with all filtering enabled: segment images, filter text-heavy blocks, drop images matching page text, and remove images overlapping text blocks for the cleanest output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production-quality extraction with all noise reduction features active

output_examples.input: A research paper PDF with text, charts, and a data table.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file with the paper title as the main heading, per-page sections with page numbers, extracted and normalized paragraphs of text, and linked image references for charts and tables.

output_examples.input: A financial report PDF with text and screenshot images.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file containing only the report text and meaningful visuals. Text-heavy screenshot images are automatically filtered out, preserving only charts and graphs.

output_examples.input: A multi-page PDF with mixed content: paragraphs, tables, and diagrams.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file organized by page with extracted text in clean paragraphs and segmented image blocks for each table and diagram, each with a unique filename for easy reference.

best_practices: Use the --help flag first to verify the script path is available before running full extraction.
Start with default settings and enable filters one at a time to understand their effect on the output.
Set an appropriate DPI between 150 and 300 based on the need for image quality versus file size.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for PDFs containing sensitive information without verifying output file security.
Do not attempt to extract text from scanned document images without first running OCR on the PDF.
Do not use extremely high DPI values above 600 for large PDFs as this creates very large image files.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support as input?
faq.answer: This skill supports PDF files as input only.

faq.question: Can this skill extract text from scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: No, it extracts selectable text only. Scanned PDFs need an OCR text layer first.

faq.question: What is the difference between the three image modes?
faq.answer: Page mode renders full pages as images. Segment mode renders detected content blocks like tables and figures. Embedded mode extracts images from the PDF content stream directly.

faq.question: Do I need to install Tesseract OCR to use all features?
faq.answer: Tesseract is required only when using the --filter-text or --filter-match options for text-heavy image filtering.

faq.question: What happens if the PDF has no selectable text?
faq.answer: The Markdown output will show 'No text extracted' for each empty page, but page images will still be rendered.

faq.question: Can this skill process multiple PDF files at the same time?
faq.answer: No, this skill processes one PDF file at a time. Each file must be processed in a separate run.
